The Hague · Est. 2023 · 32-Shaft Handloom

A woven embrace
that says it all.

Every piece from Tessura Atelier begins with a thread and ends as something you give to someone you love — or keep close on a difficult day. Handwoven in Den Haag from alpaca, yak, cashmere, and silk. Made to endure.

Explore the collection Commission a piece
Scroll

Why we weave

I see you.
I value you.
This was made for you.

A textile is not merely functional. When you wrap yourself in something handwoven — something made thread by thread, decision by decision — you are held by the intention behind it. That is what Tessura is about.

We believe objects can carry emotion across time. A blanket gifted at a birth, a throw chosen for a memorial, a piece commissioned for a wedding anniversary — these become witnesses to the moments that define us.

Every warp is set with purpose. Every weft carries meaning. The loom does not forget.

Rooted in tradition
Crafted with intention
Designed to endure
Made to be given

2026 Collection

Woven from light,
kept for life.

The 2026 Impressionist collection draws from the light of Monet, the quiet streets of Pissarro, and the riverside afternoons of Sisley — translated into structure drafts for the 32-shaft Louet Megado. Each piece is produced in a numbered edition of no more than three.

Alpaca · Yak · Edition of 3

From €820

Alpaca · Cashmere · Edition of 3

The Seine at Argenteuil

From €750

Each piece is woven to order. Lead time is 4–6 weeks.   Request a piece →

The making

Every thread
is a decision.

01

Design

Each piece begins as a structure draft — a threading sequence for 32 shafts that determines how light will move through the cloth.

02

Warp

Premium fibres — alpaca from Peru, yak from Mongolia, cashmere from the highlands, silk — are measured and dressed onto the loom by hand.

03

Weave

Each row is woven individually on the Louet Megado. A single blanket takes between 20 and 40 hours of active weaving.

04

Finish

Hems are hand-stitched. Each piece is washed, blocked, and inspected before being wrapped and numbered.

Bespoke weaving

Something
made only
for them.

A commissioned piece from Tessura is a deeply personal object. It can mark a birth, a marriage, a goodbye, a thank you — any moment that deserves to be remembered in textile form.

We work with you to translate your intention into a threading draft: choosing the fibre, the weight, the structure, the palette. The result is a piece that could not exist for anyone else.

Lead time 4–6 weeks from confirmation
Fibres Alpaca, yak, cashmere, silk, and blends
Formats Blankets, throws, and wall pieces
Location Atelier visits by appointment · Den Haag
Starting from €580 depending on size and fibre
